# Pondhawk.Logging

> A standalone .NET structured logging API by Pond Hawk Technologies built on Serilog (method tracing, object/typed-payload logging, [Sensitive] masking) plus an `ILoggerSource` acquisition seam — and a Watch Server provider (Serilog sink, Channel batching, dynamic switch-based level control). Two packages, both target net10.0, no dependency on other Pondhawk packages.

## Packages

### Pondhawk.Logging — logging API + ILoggerSource

Install: `dotnet add package Pondhawk.Logging`

**Key types (`Pondhawk.Logging` namespace):**
- `SerilogExtensions` — extensions on `Serilog.ILogger`:
  - `EnterMethod()` → disposable method-trace scope (auto entry/exit + elapsed)
  - `Inspect(name, value)` → logs `"{Name} = {Value}"` at Debug
  - `LogObject(value)` / `LogObject(title, value)` → serialize an object to a JSON payload
  - `LogJson` / `LogSql` / `LogXml` / `LogYaml` / `LogText(title, content)` → typed payloads with syntax-highlight hints
- `ILoggerSource` — inject to obtain category-scoped loggers: `CreateLogger<T>()` / `CreateLogger(Type)` / `CreateLogger(string)`, all returning `Serilog.ILogger`
- `SerilogLoggerSource` — the canonical default (`root.ForContext(SourceContext, category)`)
- `[Sensitive]` — masks a property when an object is serialized
- `CorrelationManager`, `PayloadType`, `JsonObjectSerializer`, `LogPropertyNames` (the `Pondhawk.*` property-name contract sinks read)

```csharp
using Pondhawk.Logging;

using var _ = logger.EnterMethod();            // ILogger is Serilog.ILogger
logger.Inspect(nameof(orderId), orderId);
logger.LogObject("order", order);
logger.LogSql("query", sqlText);
```
**No sink or transport** — this package is Serilog-only; a provider (e.g. Pondhawk.Logging.Watch) supplies the sink and a smarter `ILoggerSource`, or an app implements its own.

### Pondhawk.Logging.Watch — Watch Server provider

Install: `dotnet add package Pondhawk.Logging.Watch`
```csharp
using Pondhawk.Logging.Watch;
using Serilog;

Log.Logger = new LoggerConfiguration()
    .UseWatch("http://localhost:11000", "MyApp")   // Watch Server controls levels via switches
    .CreateLogger();
```
**Key types (`Pondhawk.Logging.Watch` namespace):**
- `WatchSinkExtensions` — `UseWatch(url, domain)` (primary; sets `MinimumLevel.Verbose()`) and `WriteTo.Watch(url, domain)`; out-param overloads (`UseWatch(..., out SwitchSource)`) expose the switch source
- `WatchSink` — `ILogEventSink` with unbounded `Channel<T>` batching and a circuit breaker; events serialized as MemoryPack+Brotli
- `WatchLoggerSource` — switch-aware `ILoggerSource` (skips payload serialization for switch-dropped categories)
- `SwitchSource` / `SwitchDef` / `WatchSwitchSource` — dynamic per-category level control (longest-prefix match); `WatchSwitchSource` polls a Watch Server
- `LogEvent` / `LogEventBatch` — the wire event model

## Docs
- [README.md](README.md) — overview and examples
- [src/Pondhawk.Logging/README.md](src/Pondhawk.Logging/README.md), [src/Pondhawk.Logging.Watch/README.md](src/Pondhawk.Logging.Watch/README.md) — NuGet package readmes
- [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md) plus per-project `src/*/CLAUDE.md` — repository/build guidance

## Technical details
- Both packages target `net10.0`
- XML documentation ships inside the NuGet packages (100% public-API coverage; consumers get IntelliSense)
- `Pondhawk.Logging.Watch` is consumed by the pondhawk/watch-server client
- MIT licensed; extracted from the pondhawktech/tools monorepo
